calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Continued To Aug-21-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MEL BRACKEN, READISUITE LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration.

Defendants Mel Bracken and ReadiSuite LLC's motion to compel arbitration and to stay litigation pending arbitration is GRANTED. Per the Dispute Resolution Protocol (DRP) in the TriNet Terms and Conditions Agreement (TCA), the Federal Arbitration Act applies to any dispute arising out of plaintiff's employment with ReadiSuite. DRP 9(a) expressly identifies ReadiSuite as a third-party beneficiary that may enforce the arbitration provision.
Defendants did not waive arbitration; they pled an arbitration agreement as their first affirmative defense. Further, defendants sought to stay discovery pending this motion, and plaintiff has not been materially prejudiced by the slight delay.

Whether the DRP is a contract of adhesion is disputed, but the burden is on the party opposing arbitration. (Green Tree Financial Corp.-Alabama v. Randolph (2000) 531 U.S. 79, 91-92.) Plaintiff does not carry that burden; his declaration conflicts with TriNet's regarding an option to reject the TCA and DRP terms. The screenshot showing an "Accept" button is irrelevant as it does not pertain to the TCA or DRP. An arbitration agreement is not unenforceable because it is an employment condition. (McManus v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 92, 92-103.) The TCA is only five pages long; the DRP was marked with a bolded header; a link was presented to JAMS Rules; and the TCA was provided to plaintiff as an email attachment for review and he never objected to its terms.

The PAGA waiver's alleged substantive unconscionability is irrelevant as plaintiff makes no PAGA claim. Likewise, the unilateral modification term is not at issue. The confidentiality term is not unconscionable as it allows for disclosures if required by law. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
